This file is the promotion payload, not evidence that `v0.3.0` is public.
Publication is established only by the immutable GitHub Release metadata and
its exact verified assets.

Veqtor v0.3.0 is a community-supported technical Alpha focused on verifiable
local contract-review workflows and easier activation in Claude Desktop for
macOS.

Highlights:

- the eight-tool MCP surface adds bounded `inspect_document` views over a
  mechanical accepted/current main-body projection and seed-centred
  `map_rounds` evidence maps;
- Round Map reports complete exact paragraph equality, explicit
  unresolved/ambiguous states and recorded document-byte derivations without
  claiming chronology, paragraph lineage, legal effect or authorship;
- decision-record locking, full-frame writes and rollback are hardened for
  bounded cross-thread/process contention, partial writes, storage failure and
  workspace-path replacement;
- a versioned macOS Claude Desktop Extension (`.mcpb`) with explicit
  tracked-change-author configuration, all eight tools, a deterministic
  four-round demo and a ready-to-run inspection/map prompt;
- the proof-bound preflight/apply flow detects changes to the source, edit
  request, author or predicted output between the two calls;
- reproducible wheel, source archive and MCPB builds. `SHA256SUMS.txt` binds all
  three release payloads; the checksum is an integrity check, not a digital
  signature.

The first extension activation may download a compatible Python runtime and
the dependencies pinned in `uv.lock` through Claude Desktop's UV runtime. The
extension is a macOS activation path; version-pinned `uvx` and `uv tool
install` remain available for supported command-line installations.

To install in Claude Desktop, download and open
`veqtor-mcp-0.3.0-macos.mcpb`, approve the extension, and enter the name that
Word should show as the author of new tracked changes. Then run the bundled
`try_veqtor_demo` prompt.

For an optional advanced integrity check, compare the output of
`shasum -a 256 veqtor-mcp-0.3.0-macos.mcpb` with its entry in
`SHA256SUMS.txt`. The MCPB is not digitally signed. Because this is the first
public MCPB, there is no older extension to roll back to; this release tests
clean install, uninstall, post-uninstall tool absence and same-artifact
reinstall. Real upgrade and rollback become release gates for the next MCPB.

Veqtor remains a local technical Alpha. It is not legal advice, a hosted
service, a full Word editor, a chronology/lineage proof system or a
tamper-evident audit log. Review the
[known limitations](https://github.com/JohnDeer-ai/veqtor-mcp/blob/v0.3.0/KNOWN_LIMITATIONS.md)
before using it on a real matter.
